attractions.name: Acadia National Park
attractions.description: Acadia National Park protects the natural beauty of the highest rocky headlands along the Atlantic coastline of the United States. It encompasses more than 48,000 acres, including Mount Desert Island, the Schoodic Peninsula, and Isle au Haut. Visitors enjoy a rich landscape of granite peaks, historic carriage roads, and rugged coastal views. NPS Overview
attractions.hours: Park: Open 24 hours daily. Hulls Cove Visitor Center (May 1–Oct 31): 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Sand Beach Entrance Station: Open 24 hours (not always manned). NPS Hours, Apple Maps
attractions.days_open: Open year-round, seven days a week. NPS Operating Hours
attractions.admission: Private Vehicle ($35.00), Motorcycle ($30.00), Per Person ($20.00). Note: Vehicle reservations are required for Cadillac Summit Road ($6.00). Non-US residents (16+) must pay an additional $100 fee. NPS Fees
highlights: 'Cadillac Mountain Summit for sunrise views [NPS', '27-mile Park Loop Road scenic drive NPS', '45 miles of historic stone-bridged Carriage Roads Bar Harbor', 'Jordan Pond and its famous popovers at Jordan Pond House AllTrips', 'Thunder Hole for dramatic ocean waves NPS', 'Challenging iron-rung hikes like the Beehive and Precipice Trails Noahawaii']
attractions.tips: Buy your entrance pass online in advance to save time. Vehicle reservations are required for Cadillac Summit Road from late May through October. Visit during 'shoulder' seasons like late spring or early autumn for smaller crowds and vibrant foliage. NPS Planning
attractions.nearby: Bar Harbor Village, Bass Harbor Head Lighthouse, Schoodic Peninsula, and the Wild Gardens of Acadia. Acadia Magic

attractions.name: Austin Street Brewery, Austin Street Brewery
attractions.description: Austin Street Brewery, founded in 2013 by Jake Austin and Will Fisher, is a staple of the Portland craft beer community known for its creative independence and high-quality brews (Maine Brewers' Guild). The brewery operates two locations, including a spacious flagship taproom in East Bayside and its original production facility on Industrial Way, offering a laid-back atmosphere for both beer enthusiasts and families (Austin Street Brewery).
attractions.address: 115 Fox St, Portland, ME 04101, Austin Street Brewery
attractions.hours: Sunday: 12:00PM - 6:00PM; Monday - Thursday: 2:00PM - 8:00PM; Friday - Saturday: 12:00PM - 8:00PM, Austin Street Brewery
highlights: 'Flagship Patina Pale Ale and a diverse, rotating lineup of IPAs, stouts, and sours ([Austin Street Brewery)', 'Two distinct Portland locations: the modern East Bayside taproom and the original Industrial Way space (Hop Culture)', 'Dog and family-friendly environment featuring indoor and outdoor seating and board games (Bringfido, So Much Life)', 'Daily food truck schedule featuring local Portland vendors (Tripadvisor)', 'Inclusion of non-beer options like wine, hard cider, and craft cocktails (Boozing Abroad)', "100% independent ownership maintained by the original founders (Maine Brewers' Guild)"]
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to check the daily food vendor schedule on the brewery's website to see which local food trucks are on-site (Tripadvisor). The Fox Street location is particularly popular for its expansive patio and proximity to other Bayside breweries, so arriving early on weekends is recommended (WGME).
attractions.nearby: Rising Tide Brewing Company, Lone Pine Brewing Company, Goodfire Brewing Co., and the Bayside Trail, BEER IN THE CITY, Reddit

attractions.name: Back Cove Trail (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Back Cove Trail is a popular 3.6-mile multi-use loop in Portland, Maine, that circumnavigates the scenic Back Cove estuary (Wikipedia). The trail is primarily flat with a mix of paved and stone dust surfaces, offering panoramic views of the city skyline and connecting to other major trail systems like the Eastern Promenade (Portland Trails). It is one of the oldest and most popular trails in the city, frequently used for walking, running, and biking (Maine Trail Finder).
attractions.hours: Monday–Sunday: 6AM–10PM (Wanderlog)
highlights: '3.6-mile loop trail ideal for walking, running, and biking ([Portland Trails)', 'Panoramic views of the Portland skyline and Casco Bay (Komoot)', 'Distance markers posted every quarter mile and kilometer (Maine Trail Finder)', "Connections to Bayside and Eastern Promenade Trails under Tukey's Bridge (Wikipedia)", 'Fully accessible for people with disabilities and strollers (Maine By Foot)', 'Dog-friendly with on-leash access and seasonal water fountains (Wanderlog)']
attractions.tips: Be mindful of the tides, as the cove is an estuary that can emit a strong smell at low tide (Tripadvisor). Most visitors prefer walking the loop in a clockwise direction starting from the Preble Street Extension parking lot (Wanderlog). While the trail is mostly flat, there is a slight hill on the stretch paralleling I-295 (Maine Trail Finder).
attractions.nearby: Payson Park, Eastern Promenade, Bayside Trail, East End Beach, and the Maine Narrow Gauge Railroad Co. & Museum (Komoot, Wanderlog)

attractions.name: Bissell Brothers Brewing Company
attractions.description: Founded in 2013, Bissell Brothers Brewing Company is a cornerstone of Maine's craft beer culture, located in a 100-year-old former railcar repair building on Thompson's Point. They are dedicated to pushing the boundaries of craft beer while staying deeply rooted in their home state, offering style-defining ales alongside locally inspired dishes. Bissell Brothers
attractions.hours: Mon: 11am – 5pm, Tue: 11am – 5pm, Wed: 11am – 8pm, Thu: 11am – 8pm, Fri: 11am – 8pm, Sat: 11am – 8pm, Sun: 11am – 6pm Bissell Brothers
highlights: 'Flagship IPA "The Substance Ale" brewed with 100% Maine-grown grain [Bissell Brothers', 'Bustling taproom and kitchen located in a historic 100-year-old former railway building Bissell Brothers', 'Regularly released specialty beers like "Swish" and "Nothing Gold" Bissell Brothers', 'Community-focused events including trivia nights, run clubs, and live music Bissell Brothers', 'Full-service kitchen featuring seasonal sandwiches, salads, and locally sourced snacks Bissell Brothers', 'Family-friendly atmosphere with a variety of non-alcoholic options like hop water and local sodas Bissell Brothers']
attractions.tips: Parking on Thompson's Point is managed by a third party and requires payment via kiosks or the Passport app (Zone 1213). For a car-free visit, the brewery is a 7-minute walk from the Portland Transportation Center or accessible via the Fore River Trail and Greater Portland Metro bus service. Please note that only service animals are allowed inside the taproom or on the patio. Bissell Brothers
attractions.nearby: Portland Transportation Center (Amtrak/Concord Trailways), Fore River Trail, Stroudwater Distillery, Thompson's Point Event Venue, Children's Museum & Theatre of Maine Tripadvisor, Bissell Brothers

attractions.name: Browne Trading Gourmet Market
attractions.description: Browne Trading Gourmet Market is a premier specialty food destination located in Portland's historic Old Port. Founded by Rod Browne Mitchell, the market is a "foodie’s paradise" offering elite-quality seafood, one-of-a-kind caviar, and a highly curated selection of artisanal cheeses, smoked fish, and gourmet pantry items. Browne Trading Company Blog, Browne Trading Company Market Page
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM; Saturday - Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM Browne Trading Company, Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: Daily (7 days a week) Browne Trading Company
highlights: 'Premier supplier of fine caviar, fresh seafood, and in-house smoked delicacies [Browne Trading Company', 'Largest curation of fine wines north of Boston Browne Trading Company', 'Full-service fishmonger providing custom filleting, scaling, and gutting at the counter Browne Trading Company Blog', 'Artisanal cheese and chocolate cut to order by the market team Browne Trading Company Blog', 'Daily lunch program featuring made-to-order seafood salads, rice poke bowls, and rotating soups Browne Trading Company Blog']
attractions.tips: Ask for a guided tour of the caviar program; employees are often available to provide tastings and explain the textures and tastes of different varieties. Visit for a quick bite from the lunch program and enjoy seafood specials with a view of the working waterfront. American Weekender, Browne Trading Company Blog
attractions.nearby: Old Port Waterfront, Casco Bay Lines Ferry Terminal, DiMillo's Old Port Marina, Standard Baking Co., Portland Museum of Art Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Bug Light Park
attractions.description: Bug Light Park is a scenic 8.78-acre waterfront park located at the eastern terminus of the Greenbelt Walkway, offering panoramic views of the Portland Harbor and skyline. It is most famous as the home of the Portland Breakwater Lighthouse, an elegant 1875 structure nicknamed 'Bug Light' for its diminutive size, and the Liberty Ship Memorial, which honors the 30,000 workers who built ships here during WWII. Today, the park is a popular destination for picnicking, kite flying, and salt water fishing.
attractions.hours: Daily: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free (Seasonal fees required for boat launch)
highlights: ['Portland Breakwater Lighthouse (Bug Light), built in 1875 and modeled after an ancient Greek monument.', "Liberty Ship Memorial, commemorating the site's history as a major WWII shipbuilding hub.", 'Panoramic views of the Portland harbor, skyline, and passing cruise ships.', 'The eastern terminus of the Greenbelt Walkway, a paved path for biking and walking.', 'Expansive green space ideal for kite flying, picnicking, and seasonal events like Summer Movie Nights.', 'Public boat launch area providing direct access to Casco Bay.']
attractions.tips: The park is known for its sea breezes, making it one of the best spots in the area for kite flying. Parking is free and plentiful, but restrooms are only available on a seasonal basis. Dogs are welcome but must be kept under voice control and within 100 feet.
attractions.nearby: South Portland Historical Society & Museum, Spring Point Ledge Lighthouse, Willard Beach, Portland Head Light (Cape Elizabeth)

attractions.name: Bull Moose Music (Mill Creek)
attractions.description: Bull Moose Music is a prominent independent and employee-owned entertainment retailer based in Maine, specializing in a wide array of new and used media (Wikipedia). Founded in 1989, the chain has become a local staple, offering everything from vinyl records and CDs to movies, books, and video games (Bull Moose). The stores are celebrated for their music-savvy staff and commitment to supporting local arts through showcases and events (Bull Moose).
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sunday: 9:00 AM - 10:00 PM (Bull Moose)
highlights: 'Extensive collection of new and used vinyl records and CDs ([Tripadvisor)', 'Wide variety of movies including DVDs, Blu-rays, and 4K Ultra HD (The US Sun)', 'Large selection of video games and consoles (The US Sun)', 'Diverse assortment of books, puzzles, board games, and gifts (Tripadvisor)', 'Active buy-back program for trading in used media (Bull Moose)', 'Employee-owned business with a strong focus on local community (Bull Moose)']
attractions.tips: Check the sale bins for discounted music and movies, and ask the knowledgeable staff for help tracking down hard-to-find items (Yelp). Frequent shoppers should join the loyalty program to earn points and credit toward future purchases (Bull Moose).
attractions.nearby: Casco Bay Bridge, Mill Creek Park, Casco Bay, and the Portland Museum of Art (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Camp Pennant (formerly Liquid Riot Bottling Company)
attractions.description: Located in the heart of the Old Port, Camp Pennant (formerly Liquid Riot Bottling Company) is a waterfront brewery, distillery, and restaurant inspired by nostalgic Maine summers. The venue offers house-made craft beers and spirits alongside wood-fired Neapolitan-style pizzas and fresh local seafood in a whimsical, camp-themed setting (Camp Pennant, Portland Old Port).
attractions.hours: Monday-Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 8:00 PM; Thursday-Saturday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM; Sunday: 11:30 AM – 8:00 PM (Camp Pennant)

attractions.name: Casco Bay Lines (Casco Bay Island Transit District)
attractions.description: Casco Bay Lines is a ferry system that provides year-round passenger, freight, postal, and vehicle ferry service to the islands of Casco Bay from Portland, Maine. For over 150 years, its vessels have been transporting people between the mainland and seven islands, offering both essential transit and scenic specialty cruises. Casco Bay Lines About Us
attractions.hours: Ticket Office: Monday-Sunday, 5:00 AM - 10:30 PM. (Ferry departure times vary by island and season; first trips typically begin around 5:00 AM and last trips around 11:30 PM). Casco Bay Lines Contact Us

highlights: 'Year-round ferry service to seven islands: Peaks, Little Diamond, Great Diamond, Diamond Cove, Long Island, Chebeague, and Cliff Island. [Casco Bay Lines', 'Narrated scenic cruises including the famous 3-hour "Mail Boat Run." Tripadvisor', 'Specialty excursions such as the Sunset Cruise, Moonlight Cruise, and the 2026 Cruising Casco Bay Concert Series. Casco Bay Lines Specialty Cruises', 'Pet-friendly travel (leashed dogs require a ticket; service animals travel for free). Casco Bay Lines Passenger Fares', 'Vehicle and freight transportation services to select islands. Casco Bay Lines Rates', "Modern ferry terminal located at the historic Maine State Pier in the heart of Portland's Old Port. Casco Bay Lines Parking & Directions"]

attractions.name: Coastal Maine Botanical Gardens
attractions.description: Coastal Maine Botanical Gardens is the largest botanical garden in New England, encompassing over 300 acres of rocky coastal forest and tidal shoreline. The grounds feature 17 acres of meticulously designed ornamental gardens, woodland trails, and large-scale art installations like the "Guardians of the Seeds" giant trolls (Coastal Maine Botanical Gardens).

attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes as the garden spans hundreds of acres; advance tickets are required for entry. Complimentary EV charging is available in Lot F while the gardens are open, but note that only service animals are permitted on the grounds (Coastal Maine Botanical Gardens).

attractions.name: Deering Oaks Park Wikipedia
attractions.description: Deering Oaks Park is a historic 55-acre public park in Portland, Maine, established in 1879 and listed on the National Register of Historic Places. Designed by City Engineer William Goodwin based on Frederick Law Olmsted’s master plan, the park features a naturalistic landscape with a central pond, diverse recreational facilities, and iconic structures such as the Victorian "Castle." It serves as a vibrant community hub, hosting major events like the seasonal Portland Farmers' Market and free summer theater. Wikipedia, Friends of Deering Oaks
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 6:30 AM - 10:00 PM City of Portland Code
highlights: 'The Serene Pond featuring a historic Victorian-style Duck House [Airial Travel', 'The "Castle-in-the-Park," a Richardsonian Romanesque warming hut built in 1894 Wikipedia', "Portland Farmers' Market, held on the park grounds on Wednesdays and Saturdays (seasonal) Wikipedia", 'Free Summer Shakespeare performances by the Fenix Theatre Company Fenix Theatre', 'Modernized children’s splash pad and accessible playground Airial Travel', 'Extensive athletic facilities including tennis courts, basketball courts, and a baseball diamond Wikipedia']
attractions.tips: Arrive early for the Farmers' Market on Wednesdays and Saturdays to get the best selection and beat the crowds. Parking can be limited during major events, so consider exploring side streets or using public transportation. The park is especially photogenic during the fall foliage season and spring flower blooms. Airial Travel, Tripadvisor

Opened in 1994, the ballpark is celebrated for its old-style architecture and unique features like the 'Maine Monster,' a 37-foot replica of Fenway Park’s Green Monster. Wikipedia, MiLB

attractions.name: Duckfat (The Original)
attractions.description: Duckfat is a small, family-run European-style sandwich shop and bistro renowned for its hand-cut Belgian frites fried in duck fat. Since 2005, it has been a local institution in Portland, serving decadent comfort food like panini, poutine, and house-made milkshakes in a cozy, communal atmosphere.
attractions.hours: Sunday–Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM; Friday–Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM
highlights: ['Signature Belgian frites twice-fried in duck fat and served in a simple cone', 'Duckfat Poutine featuring local Pineland Farms cheese curds and rich duck gravy', 'House-made craft sodas and milkshakes created with local Gelato Fiasco gelato', 'James Beard Award-winning chef and owner Rob Evans at the helm', 'Locally sourced Maine Norwis potatoes from Green Thumb Farms', 'Rustic and cozy interior with a friendly, communal dining experience']
attractions.tips: Duckfat does not accept reservations and often has long wait times; arrive early or visit during off-peak hours to secure a table. For a faster alternative, the Duckfat Frites Shack walk-up window is just a 15-minute walk away. Be sure to try the sauce flight to sample all five house-made mayos and ketchups with your frites.

attractions.description: Eventide Oyster Co. is a modern revival of the great American oyster bar, offering pristine shellfish and a fresh take on classic New England fare. Located in Portland's Old Port district, the restaurant is renowned for its Maine granite oyster display and its James Beard Award-winning menu. It is particularly famous for its unique Brown Butter Lobster Roll served on a steamed bao-style bun. Eventide Oyster Co., Big Tree Hospitality

It is celebrated for its charming cobblestone streets, 19th-century brick architecture, and a dense concentration of independent boutiques, galleries, and award-winning restaurants. (Wikipedia), New England Wanderlust)

attractions.name: Fore River Sanctuary
attractions.description: Fore River Sanctuary is an 85-acre nature preserve that serves as a tranquil oasis within the city of Portland. It is home to Jewell Falls, the city's only natural waterfall, and features diverse habitats such as salt and freshwater marshes, meadows, and forests. Visitors can also explore historic remains of the Cumberland and Oxford Canal while enjoying over five miles of multi-use trails. (Portland Trails, Maine Trail Finder)
attractions.hours: Dawn to Dusk daily (Portland Trails, Beyond The Moments)
highlights: ["Jewell Falls, Portland's only natural waterfall", 'Historic Cumberland and Oxford Canal towpath and repair basin', 'Diverse birdwatching opportunities in salt and freshwater marshlands', 'Over 5.6 miles of interconnected trails for hiking and mountain biking', 'Dog-friendly trails with designated off-leash areas']
attractions.tips: There are no public restrooms on-site, so plan accordingly. Parking is available at several trailheads, but spaces are limited; ensure you follow all posted signs, especially at the Maine Orthopaedic Center lot. Trails can be slippery around the waterfall after rain, and it is recommended to bring water and insect repellent during the summer months. (Beyond The Moments, Maine Trail Finder)
attractions.nearby: Tate House Museum, Stroudwater River, Thompson's Point, Portland Jetport, and the Old Port district. (Beyond The Moments, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Fore Street Restaurant
attractions.description: Fore Street is a landmark dining destination in Portland's Old Port, known for its focus on locally sourced ingredients from Maine's farmers and fishermen (Visit Portland). The restaurant features a unique open kitchen with a brick and soapstone hearth, where seafood, meats, and vegetables are prepared over hardwood fires (Official Website). Since opening in 1996, it has earned national acclaim and multiple James Beard Award nominations (Wikipedia)).

attractions.name: Fort Preble
attractions.description: Fort Preble is a historic military fortification built in 1808 to protect Portland Harbor from British attack during the Embargo Act and the War of 1812. The fort was active through all major U.S. wars until 1950 and now resides on the campus of Southern Maine Community College (SMCC), where its remains and scenic coastal views are accessible to the public. Southern Maine Community College, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: 6:00 AM - 9:00 PM daily South Portland Official Website

Established in the late 19th century and operating under its current name since 1966, it offers an astounding array of fresh local specialties like lobster, haddock, and cod alongside exotic varieties from around the globe. attractions.name: Kennebunkport (via Kennebunk-Kennebunkport-Arundel Chamber of Commerce)
attractions.description: Kennebunkport is a quintessential New England seaside town known for its historic shipbuilding roots, vibrant Dock Square shopping district, and as the summer home of the Bush family. The Kennebunk-Kennebunkport-Arundel Chamber of Commerce serves as the primary visitor resource, offering guides to the area's pristine beaches, world-class dining, and scenic coastal trails. Visitors can explore working fishing villages, grand historic mansions, and a variety of maritime activities along the Kennebunk River.

It serves as a vibrant cultural and commercial hub, featuring an eclectic mix of independent boutiques, award-winning restaurants, and lively bars overlooking the working fishing piers of Fore River. Visitors can enjoy a blend of maritime history and modern amenities, making it the city's premier destination for shopping, dining, and nightlife. (Wikipedia, Portland Old Port)

attractions.name: Wharf Street
attractions.description: Wharf Street is a historic, pedestrian-only cobblestone street located in the heart of Portland's Old Port district. Renowned for its 19th-century brick architecture and uneven quarried granite blocks, it serves as a vibrant hub for upscale seafood restaurants, rustic pubs, and lively nightlife. The street's distinctive second-story "loading doors" are a testament to its maritime past, when ships could sail directly up to the buildings to unload cargo before the area was expanded via landfill. (Elizabeth Smith Knits, Portland Old Port)
attractions.hours: Public street open 24/7; peak activity typically 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM daily (Visit Portland)

attractions.tips: Wear comfortable, flat shoes as the historic cobblestones are famously uneven and wobbly. Visit in the evening to enjoy the glowing cafe lights and lively nightlife atmosphere. For popular restaurants like Street & Co., it is highly recommended to make reservations well in advance. (Tripadvisor, Travel Bliss Now)

Built in 1785–1786 for General Peleg Wadsworth, the house has been meticulously restored to its mid-nineteenth-century character and remains furnished with original family heirlooms. It serves as a significant architectural artifact and a memorial to the Longfellow family's legacy in American literature and history. Maine Historical Society

attractions.tips: Dogs are allowed off-leash in designated areas before 9 AM and after 5 PM in summer, and all day in winter. Public transportation is available via Metro buses #1 and #8. Visit Fort Allen Park for the best historical monuments and views.

attractions.name: Eastern Promenade Trail
attractions.description: The Eastern Promenade Trail is a scenic 2.1-mile waterfront multi-use path built along an old rail corridor, offering spectacular views of Casco Bay and Portland Harbor. It connects downtown Portland to the Back Cove and Bayside Trails, passing by East End Beach and historic sites within the 68-acre Eastern Promenade park. (Portland Trails, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM (Daily) (Waze, Islands.com)
attractions.phone: (207) 775-2411 (Portland Trails office) (Portland Trails) or (207) 756-8275 (East End Beach/City) (Waze)
highlights: 'Spectacular views of Casco Bay and Portland Harbor ([Portland Trails)', 'Access to East End Beach for swimming and boat launching (Friends of the Eastern Promenade)', 'Connections to Back Cove and Bayside Trails (Maine Trail Finder)', 'Historic sites including Fort Allen Park and the mast of USS Portland (Wikipedia)', 'Multi-use paved and stone dust paths for walking, biking, and running (Portland Trails)']
attractions.tips: Dogs are allowed on leash on the trail; however, they are only allowed on East End Beach during the off-season or specific hours in summer. Parking is available at the Commercial Street and Cutter Street trailheads. (Islands.com, Maine Trail Finder)
attractions.nearby: East End Beach, Fort Allen Park, Maine Narrow Gauge Railroad Museum, Back Cove Trail (Portland Trails, Maine Trail Finder)

attractions.name: Maine Craft Distilling
attractions.description: Maine Craft Distilling is a farm-to-flask distillery located in the East End of Portland, Maine, that has been producing award-winning spirits since 2012. The venue features a Public House where visitors can enjoy handcrafted cocktails and local food, alongside a distillery space that hosts regular live music and community events. Their commitment to using local ingredients and sustainable practices is reflected in their diverse lineup of spirits, including whiskey, gin, and blueberry liquor. Maine Craft Distilling About, Tripadvisor
attractions.address: 123 Washington Ave, Portland, Maine 04101 Maine Craft Distilling
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday - Thursday: 4:00 PM - 10:00 PM; Friday - Saturday: 12:00 PM - 10:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM - 8:00 PM (Kitchen closes at 9:00 PM Tue-Sat and 6:00 PM Sun) Maine Craft Distilling
attractions.days_open: Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday Maine Craft Distilling
attractions.admission: Free entry to the Public House; Live Music Events typically $20 Advance / $25 Day of Maine Craft Distilling Events
highlights: 'Farm-to-flask spirits crafted using local Maine ingredients since 2012 [Maine Craft Distilling', 'Award-winning spirit lineup including Fifty Stone Single Malt Whiskey and Blueshine Blueberry Liquor Maine Craft Distilling Spirits', 'Public House serving handcrafted cocktails, local beer, and a full food menu Maine Craft Distilling', 'Regular live music and public events held in a unique distillery venue space Maine Craft Distilling Events', 'Daily Happy Hour from 4PM to 6PM on weekdays Maine Craft Distilling']
attractions.tips: Check their social media or website for live music schedules as some events require tickets. Take advantage of the weekday Happy Hour from 4PM-6PM for drink specials. Note that the kitchen closes earlier than the bar (9PM Tue-Sat, 6PM Sun). Maine Craft Distilling
attractions.nearby: Eastern Promenade Trail, Portland Observatory, Casco Bay, East Bayside breweries (Rising Tide, Austin Street), and the Old Port district. Tripadvisor, Adventurous Kate

attractions.name: Munjoy Hill Neighborhood
attractions.description: Munjoy Hill is a historic and scenic neighborhood located at the northeastern end of Portland's peninsula, overlooking Casco Bay and downtown. Known as "The Hill," it features a unique blend of 19th-century worker cottages, grand Victorian homes, and the expansive 68-acre Eastern Promenade park. (Munjoy Hill Neighborhood Organization, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 92 Congress Street, Portland, ME 04101 (Munjoy Hill Neighborhood Organization) / 138 Congress Street, Portland, ME 04101 (Portland Observatory)
attractions.hours: Neighborhood: Open 24/7; Portland Observatory: Seasonal (May–Oct), typically 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM daily (Greater Portland Landmarks)
attractions.admission: Free (Neighborhood); Portland Observatory: Adults $10, Seniors/Military/Students $8, Kids (6-16) $5, Kids (under 6) Free (Greater Portland Landmarks)
highlights: ['Eastern Promenade (scenic waterfront park with walking trails)', 'Portland Observatory (historic 1807 maritime signal tower)', "East End Beach (Portland's only public beach on the peninsula)", 'Fort Allen Park (featuring historic monuments and panoramic harbor views)', 'Munjoy Hill Historic District (well-preserved 19th-century architecture)', 'Maine Narrow Gauge Railroad & Museum (historic train rides along the shore)']
attractions.tips: Street parking is available but can be competitive; check local signage for restrictions. Visit the Loring Memorial for exceptional sunset views over the city, and note that dogs are permitted on East End Beach after 5:00 PM. (Portland Discovery, Wikipedia)
attractions.nearby: Old Port District, Casco Bay Ferry Terminal, Back Cove Trail, Portland Downtown (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Portland Observatory
attractions.description: Built in 1807, the Portland Observatory is the only known surviving maritime signal tower in the United States and is designated as a National Historic Landmark. Visitors can climb the 86-foot octagonal timber-frame tower to enjoy panoramic views of Casco Bay and the city skyline while learning about its history as a ship-to-shore communication hub. Wikipedia, Official Website
attractions.hours: May 23–May 31: Daily 10:00 AM – 3:30 PM; June 1–August 31: Sunday–Wednesday 10:00 AM – 3:30 PM, Thursday–Saturday 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM; September 1–October 12: Daily 10:00 AM – 3:30 PM. Official Website
attractions.days_open: Seasonal (Late May to Mid-October); open daily during peak season with extended summer hours. Official Website
attractions.admission: Non-Residents: Adults $10, Seniors/Students/Military $8, Kids (6-16) $5, Kids under 6 Free. Portland Residents: Adults $5, Kids (6-16) $3. Official Website
highlights: ['Climb 104 stairs across seven stories of historic timber-frame architecture.', 'Panoramic views of Portland, Casco Bay, and the distant White Mountains from the cupola balcony.', "45-minute guided tours detailing the life of Captain Lemuel Moody and the tower's maritime significance.", 'Designated as both a National Historic Landmark and a National Civil Engineering Landmark.', 'Interactive exhibits on signal flag communication and 19th-century maritime trade.']
attractions.tips: The tower is not handicap accessible due to its historic spiral staircases; comfortable walking shoes are highly recommended. It is advisable to book guided tours in advance as group sizes are limited to 10 people. Portland residents should bring ID to receive discounted admission. Official Website
attractions.nearby: Eastern Promenade, East End Beach, Maine Narrow Gauge Railroad Museum, Victoria Mansion, and the historic Old Port district. TripAdvisor

attractions.name: Rabelais Inc. (Fine Books on Food & Drink) Rabelais Inc.
attractions.description: Rabelais Inc. is a renowned antiquarian bookstore specializing in rare and hard-to-find books, manuscripts, and ephemera related to the culture of food and drink. Founded in 2006, the business offers an unparalleled inventory spanning six centuries of culinary history, from classic cookbooks to specialized works on winemaking and cocktail culture Rabelais Inc..
attractions.address: 93 Washington Ave (Former Pop-up Location) / PO Box 1298 (Mailing)
attractions.hours: Retail location closed; now operating online and by appointment Rabelais Inc.

attractions.tips: Check the official website for upcoming appearances at international book fairs and sign up for their newsletter for rare item arrivals Rabelais Inc..
attractions.nearby: The Black Box (Washington Ave), Moonday Coffee, Inner North End restaurants Portland Food Map

attractions.description: Acorn Productions is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization dedicated to nurturing and developing the performing arts in Greater Portland. They produce annual festivals like the Maine Playwrights Festival and operate the Acorn Acting Academy, providing educational programs and workshops for artists of all levels. Source: About Page

attractions.days_open: Varies by event/program (typically Sunday-Monday for classes, full festival weeks in May). Source: Classes Page, Source: Events Page
attractions.admission: Pay What You Can (Maine Playwrights Festival); Free/Donations welcome (High School Playwrights Festival); Classes: $85 - $150. Source: Events Page, Source: Classes Page

Source: Events Page']
attractions.tips: Tickets for the Maine Playwrights Festival are offered on a 'Pay What You Can' basis to lower economic barriers. Acting classes are held at Mayo Street Arts (10 Mayo St) and some events at Stevens Square Theatre. Source: Events Page, Source: Classes Page

attractions.name: Allagash Brewing Company
attractions.description: Allagash Brewing Company is an independent, B Corp-certified brewery specializing in Belgian-inspired beers. Founded in 1995 by James Beard Award winner Rob Tod, it is home to the world-renowned Allagash White and offers a spacious, family-friendly tasting room with local food truck pairings.
attractions.hours: Thursday-Saturday: 11am-8pm; Sunday-Monday: 11am-7pm; Tuesday-Wednesday: Closed. Allagash Hours
attractions.days_open: Monday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Closed Tuesday-Wednesday)

attractions.name: Deering Center Neighborhood
attractions.description: Deering Center is a vibrant, "village-like" neighborhood in Portland, Maine, known for its historic homes, independent shops, and extensive greenspaces. It serves as a community hub with a walkable "micro-downtown" and schools ranging from kindergarten through high school. (Deering Center Neighborhood Association, Portland Old Port)
attractions.hours: Neighborhood: Open 24/7; Parks (Baxter Woods/Evergreen Cemetery): Dawn to dusk; Association Meetings: 1st Monday of most months, 7:00 PM – 8:30 PM (Deering Center Neighborhood Association, Portland Trails)
attractions.days_open: Open daily; Association meetings on the first Monday of most months (September–June) (Deering Center Neighborhood Association)
highlights: 'Evergreen Cemetery: A historic 239-acre garden-style cemetery on the National Register of Historic Places ([Wikipedia))', 'Baxter Woods: A 30-acre nature preserve with shaded trails for walking and biking (Portland Trails)', 'Deering Center Village: A two-block stretch of Stevens Avenue featuring local businesses like Rwanda Bean Coffee and The Honey Exchange (Down East Magazine)', 'Annual Porchfest: A popular community music festival where local bands perform on neighborhood porches (City of Portland)', 'Educational Hub: Home to Longfellow Elementary, Lincoln Middle, and Deering High Schools (Maine Home Connection)']
attractions.tips: Visit Evergreen Cemetery during the fall for peak foliage and birdwatching opportunities. Explore the independent boutiques and cafes on Stevens Avenue to experience the neighborhood's unique local culture. (Down East Magazine, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Hot Suppa! (Hot Suppa)
attractions.description: Established in 2006 by two brothers in an 1860 brick Victorian on Portland's West End, Hot Suppa! specializes in American Southern classics made from scratch. The restaurant features high ceilings, brick walls, and original moldings, providing a unique and comfortable setting for its popular all-day brunch and Southern fare (Hot Suppa).
attractions.hours: Mon: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM, Tue: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM, Wed: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM, Thu: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM, Fri: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM, Sat: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM, Sun: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM (Hot Suppa)

attractions.name: Mayor Baxter Woods Park
attractions.description: Mayor Baxter Woods Park is a 30-acre nature preserve and municipal forest located in Portland's Deering Center neighborhood. It is the largest undisturbed forested area in the city, offering a network of easy, shaded trails through towering pines and mature hardwoods. The park serves as a sanctuary for birds and a peaceful retreat for walking, biking, and nature observation.
attractions.hours: Dawn to dusk, Monday through Sunday
highlights: ['30-acre urban nature preserve with approximately 1.5 miles of easy, well-marked trails.', 'The largest undisturbed tract of forest within the city of Portland.', 'Features diverse tree species including towering pines, mature oaks, and ash.', 'Directly connects to the trails of the historic Evergreen Cemetery.', 'Popular local destination for bird watching and "forest bathing."', 'Offers seasonal off-leash dog access (typically from August 1 to March 31).']
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy footwear to navigate tree roots and rocks on side trails. Be sure to check current seasonal dog leashing rules on the city website before visiting with pets. Bug spray is recommended during the summer months due to the dense vegetation. Parking is most convenient at the small lot on Stevens Avenue or on the residential Hartley Street.
attractions.nearby: Evergreen Cemetery, Back Cove Trail, Deering Oaks Park, Payson Park, University of New England Portland Campus

attractions.name: Pai Men Miyake
attractions.description: Pai Men Miyake is a warm and friendly Japanese restaurant in Portland, Maine, focusing on authentic, hand-made ramen. The menu also features a variety of sushi rolls, gyoza, and small plates, complemented by a selection of craft beers on draft (Miyake Restaurants, Portland Old Port).
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:30am-9:00pm, Tuesday: 11:30am-9:00pm, Wednesday: 11:30am-9:00pm, Thursday: 11:30am-9:00pm, Friday: 11:30am-9:00pm, Saturday: 11:30am-9:00pm, Sunday: Closed (Official Website)
highlights: 'Authentic, hand-made Japanese ramen prepared daily ([Official Website)', 'Popular house-made steamed buns with pork belly or lobster (Official Website)', 'Extensive selection of rotating craft beers on draft (Portland Old Port)', 'Casual atmosphere perfect for quick lunches or social dinners (Nova Circle)', 'Located in the heart of Longfellow Square, a popular dining destination (Blueberry Files)']

attractions.name: Victoria Mansion (Morse-Libby House) Victoria Mansion
attractions.description: Victoria Mansion, also known as the Morse-Libby House, is a premier historic house museum and a National Historic Landmark. Built between 1858 and 1860 as a summer home for hotelier Ruggles Sylvester Morse, it is widely considered one of the finest examples of the Italian Villa style in America, retaining over 90% of its original interiors and lavish furnishings. Victoria Mansion, Visit Portland
attractions.hours: Daily: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Last admission at 3:50 PM). Closed Memorial Day, Independence Day, and Labor Day. Victoria Mansion
attractions.admission: Adults: $19.25; Senior Citizens (62+): $17.25; College Students (with ID): $8.00; Children (6-17): $6.00; Children (under 5): Free; Military: $8.00; Museums for All (SNAP/EBT): $2.00; Group Rate (10-50 people): $14.00 per person Victoria Mansion
highlights: 'Stunning Italian Villa style architecture designed by Henry Austin [Visit Portland', 'Exceptionally well-preserved original interiors and furnishings by Gustave Herter Victoria Mansion', "The magnificent and often-photographed 'flying staircase' Tripadvisor", 'Intricate decorative elements including ornate wall paintings, stained glass, and etched glass Tripadvisor', 'National Historic Landmark status awarded for its architectural significance Wikipedia']
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to book tickets in advance; allow approximately 90 minutes for a full tour. Note that there is no on-site parking, but convenient on-street parking and nearby garages are available. Victoria Mansion, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: Maine Historical Society, Wadsworth-Longfellow House, Maine Irish Heritage Center, and the Old Port district Victoria Mansion FAQ, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: West End Neighborhood
attractions.description: The West End is a historic downtown neighborhood in Portland, Maine, renowned for its remarkably preserved Victorian-era architecture and diverse community. Located primarily on Bramhall Hill, it features the scenic Western Promenade, numerous historic landmarks, and a vibrant local scene of independent shops and restaurants.
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Neighborhood); Parks: 7:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Attractions like Victoria Mansion: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM (seasonal)
attractions.admission: Free (Neighborhood access); Guided tours of sites like Victoria Mansion or Western Cemetery have fees ($6.00 - $19.25)
highlights: ['Western Promenade Park with panoramic sunset views of the White Mountains', 'Victoria Mansion, a National Historic Landmark and premier house museum', 'Western Cemetery, a historic 19th-century burial ground offering seasonal tours', 'Stunning Victorian, Italianate, and Queen Anne style residential architecture', 'Reiche Community Center, featuring a library, swimming pool, and neighborhood events']
attractions.tips: The neighborhood is best explored on foot to appreciate the intricate architectural details; parking is limited and strictly regulated. Visit the Western Promenade at sunset for the best views, and check seasonal schedules for tours of historic mansions and cemeteries.
attractions.nearby: Old Port, Arts District, Maine Medical Center, Portland Museum of Art, Thompson's Point

attractions.name: Western Promenade (Western Promenade Park)
attractions.description: Western Promenade is a historic public park and promenade laid out in 1836, perched on a scenic bluff in Portland's West End. It is renowned for its panoramic views of the Fore River and the distant White Mountains, as well as its well-preserved landscape designed by the Olmsted Brothers.
attractions.hours: 7:00 AM - 9:00 PM daily (Tripadvisor, Kupi.com)
highlights: ["Panoramic sunset views of the Fore River and New Hampshire's White Mountains", 'Historic landscape design by the Olmsted Brothers (John C. Olmsted) in 1905', 'Bronze statue of Thomas Brackett Reed, a prominent Portland statesman, erected in 1910', 'A 0.7-mile paved walking path through a National Register of Historic Places site', 'Adjacent to the historic Western Cemetery and surrounded by Victorian-era architecture']
attractions.tips: Visit during sunset for the best views. Parking is limited; try looking for spots on Pine or Brackett Streets. Note that there are no public restrooms in the park (Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Western Cemetery, Victoria Mansion, Maine Medical Center, Portland Museum of Art (Kupi.com)

attractions.name: Cape Elizabeth Light (Two Lights)
attractions.description: The Cape Elizabeth Light station, established in 1828, features two 67-foot cast-iron towers known as "Two Lights." While the west tower is a private residence, the active east tower remains a vital aid to navigation and was famously immortalized in Edward Hopper's 1929 painting "The Lighthouse at Two Lights."
attractions.address: 225 Two Lights Rd, Cape Elizabeth, ME 04107
attractions.hours: Closed to the public (Exterior viewable 24/7 from public roads and nearby parks)
attractions.admission: Free (Lighthouse and grounds are privately owned and closed to the public; exterior viewing from public areas is free)
highlights: ['Features two distinctive cast-iron towers built in 1874', 'The subject of Edward Hopper\'s famous painting "The Lighthouse at Two Lights"', 'First lighthouse to be featured on a U.S. postage stamp in 1970', 'The east tower remains an active aid to navigation with a powerful 1.8 million candlepower light', 'Located on the scenic rocky headlands of Cape Elizabeth overlooking the Atlantic Ocean']
attractions.tips: The lighthouse towers and immediate grounds are private property; please respect the residents' privacy and view only from public areas. The best vantage points for photography are at the end of Two Lights Road near Dyer Cove or from the nearby Two Lights State Park.
attractions.nearby: Two Lights State Park, Portland Head Light, Crescent Beach State Park, The Lobster Shack at Two Lights

attractions.name: Fore River Brewing Company
attractions.description: Fore River Brewing Company was a celebrated neighborhood craft brewery located in South Portland, known for its diverse selection of IPAs, stouts, and fruited sours. The brewery featured a cozy, cabin-like tasting room built with salvaged materials and a spacious outdoor backyard equipped with fire pits. Tripadvisor, Portland Food Map

[Maine Birding Field Notes', 'Large fenced-in backyard with picnic tables, rockers, and a fire pit.', 'Dog-friendly policy allowing pets both inside and outside the tasting room. New England Wanderlust']
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to bring their own food or check for scheduled food trucks as the brewery typically only offered light snacks like popcorn. It is a great spot for groups and is very welcoming to dogs. Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: Thompson's Point, Bissell Brothers Brewing, Victoria Mansion, Portland Museum of Art. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Two Lights State Park
attractions.description: Two Lights State Park encompasses 41 acres of rocky headlands on Cape Elizabeth, offering sweeping views of Casco Bay and the open Atlantic. It is home to remnants of a World War II-era coastal defense installation, including a fire control tower and bunkers. Visitors can enjoy walking trails along the shoreline, picnicking with ocean vistas, and exploring tide pools (Maine.gov, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: 9:00 a.m. to sunset daily (Maine.gov)
attractions.admission: Adult Maine Residents: $5.00; Adult Non-Residents: $7.00; Senior Non-Residents: $2.00; Maine Seniors (65+): Free; Children (5-11): $1.00; Children under 5: Free (Maine Bureau of Parks and Lands)
highlights: ['Rocky headlands with sweeping views of Casco Bay and the open Atlantic', 'Remnants of World War II-era seacoast battery and fire control tower', 'Network of shoreline trails for walking and watching ships entering Portland Harbor', 'Oceanfront picnic areas with tables and grills situated on scenic hills', 'Tide pools and rocky coast exploration areas', 'Proximity to historic twin lighthouses and the Lobster Shack restaurant']
attractions.tips: Ocean breezes can be cool even in summer, so bring warm clothing; stay at least 20 feet back from the surf on rocks for safety; arrive early on summer weekends as the parking lot fills quickly; dogs are welcome but must be on-leash (Visit Maine, Maine.gov).
attractions.nearby: Crescent Beach State Park, Kettle Cove State Park, Portland Head Light, The Lobster Shack at Two Lights (Maine Trail Finder)

attractions.name: Fort Williams Park
attractions.description: Fort Williams Park is a 90-acre public recreation area in Cape Elizabeth, Maine, that features the iconic Portland Head Light, the oldest lighthouse in the state. The park offers visitors a blend of scenic coastal views, historic military remains including several artillery batteries, and expansive green spaces for picnicking and leisure. It serves as a premier destination for both historical exploration and outdoor recreation along the Casco Bay coastline.
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise to Sunset, Tuesday: Sunrise to Sunset, Wednesday: Sunrise to Sunset, Thursday: Sunrise to Sunset, Friday: Sunrise to Sunset, Saturday: Sunrise to Sunset, Sunday: Sunrise to Sunset (Portland Head Light)
attractions.admission: Park admission is free; Museum admission is $2.00 for adults, $1.00 for children (ages 6-18), and free for children under 6 (Portland Head Light). Parking fees are $6.00 for a 2-hour minimum and $2.00 per hour thereafter (Portland Head Light).
highlights: ['Portland Head Light, the oldest lighthouse in Maine', 'Goddard Mansion ruins and historic military batteries', 'The scenic Cliff Walk Trail along the coast', "Children's Garden and Arboretum area", 'Ship Cove beach access and picnic facilities']
attractions.tips: The park operates on a carry-in/carry-out policy with no trash receptacles available. Parking fees must be paid via credit or debit card at on-site meters, and the lighthouse tower itself is only open to the public once a year on Maine Open Lighthouse Day (Portland Head Light).
attractions.nearby: Two Lights State Park, Crescent Beach State Park, Kettle Cove State Park, and the South Portland Historical Society (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Peaks Island
attractions.description: Peaks Island is the most populous island in Casco Bay and a vibrant neighborhood of Portland, Maine. Originally known as the 'Coney Island of Maine,' it offers a unique blend of historical sites, scenic ocean vistas, and a close-knit community atmosphere accessible via a short ferry ride. Casco Bay Lines, Wikipedia

highlights: ['Battery Steele (Historic WWII fortification)', 'Fifth Maine Museum & Civil War Lodge', 'The World’s Only Umbrella Cover Museum', 'Scenic 3.7-mile loop for biking and walking', 'Sandy Beach and rugged Backshore views', 'Golf cart tours of island history and landmarks']

attractions.description: Portland Head Light is a historic lighthouse in Cape Elizabeth, Maine, situated at the entrance of the shipping channel into Casco Bay. Completed in 1791, it is the oldest lighthouse in Maine and features a museum in the former Keepers' Quarters within the 90-acre Fort Williams Park. Wikipedia, Official Site
attractions.hours: Park: Sunrise to Sunset (year-round). Museum: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM (seasonal). Official Site
attractions.days_open: Daily (Park); Museum: Daily from Memorial Day to October 31/Indigenous Peoples Day (Weekends only in May & November). Official Site
attractions.admission: Museum: Adults $2.00, Children (6-18) $1.00, Children under 6 Free. Park: Free admission (parking fees apply). Official Site
highlights: ['Historic Lighthouse Tower (oldest in Maine)', "Award-winning Museum in former Keepers' Quarters", 'Stunning ocean views of Casco Bay', 'Goddard Mansion ruins within the park', 'Cliff walk and hiking trails', 'Battery Keyes and other historic fort structures']
attractions.tips: The lighthouse tower is generally not open for climbing except on Maine Open Lighthouse Day. Parking requires a fee ($6 for 2 hours) in several park lots, but overflow parking is free. The park is best visited during golden hour for photography, but note that gates close at sunset. Official Site, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: Ram Island Ledge Light, Spring Point Ledge Lighthouse, Two Lights State Park, Crescent Beach State Park, Downtown Portland. Official Site
